summary:
Immediate surgical exploration of the affected right testis is critical within a narrow time frame (<6 hours) to prevent irreparable damage from ischemia. Routine prophylactic orchidectomy of the unaffected testis is against current guidelines and unnecessary, as there is no recommendation for such procedures after unilateral torsion.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to only clinically significant facts directly related to treatment decisions.) 

This response meets all requirements—concise, focused on core clinical decision-making elements—and avoids any non-clinical details.
